subject: Docking Map UI spec committed — ready for review
body:
## Docking Map UI Spec — `4922f5e`

**File:** `.squad/decisions/docking-map-ui-spec.md`

Mira has written the full specification. Here's what's covered:

### Grip strip
- Hatched horizontal lines at the top of every dockable panel, height = corner radius (~8 px → ~4 lines)
- Lines follow the rounded corner arc (shortened at edges)
- Full-width hit area replaces the hamburger button
- `SizeAll` cursor on hover
- Hamburger (≡) removal checklist included for Lyra

### Docking Map popup
- Borderless floating window (`WindowStyle=None`, `AllowsTransparency`, `Topmost`)
- Three zones: Left (up to 2 columns) | Top (horizontal row, upper half) | Right (up to 2 columns)
- Dynamic sizing from slot minimums: 48×48 px (column slots), 64×32 px (top-row slots)
- Theme-matched colors throughout
- Closes on Escape or click-outside

### Button generation rules (A–F)
| Rule | Condition | Result |
|------|-----------|--------|
| A | Always | Source panel's slot = disabled/background |
| B | Source IS in this group | N slots, source disabled, others reorder |
| C | Source NOT in group | N+1 slots, all enabled (insert) |
| D | Group is empty | 1 full-size slot (dock here alone) |
| E | Adjacent to existing column | Expansion buttons left/right of that column |
| F | Column limit | Suppress expansion if already at 2 columns |

### 4 worked examples with ASCII diagrams
1. All panels in Top, nothing docked
2. One panel docked Left, source is a Top panel
3. Two panels in Left col 1, source is one of them (reorder)
4. Two left columns, source in col 1

### Implementation notes for Lyra
- `OnRender` override with `GuidelineSet` for pixel-snapped grip lines
- `DockingMapViewModel` data structure sketch
- `BuildDockingMap(sourcePanelId, currentLayout)` method signature
- Disabled-slot style override spec

### Open questions / v2
- Spatial anchor positioning (mouse centered over source slot)
- Panel move animation
- Touch hit-area expansion
- Keyboard navigation (Tab / arrows / Enter / Escape)

actions.label: Start implementation
actions.prompt:
Implement the Docking Map feature per the spec at `.squad/decisions/docking-map-ui-spec.md`. Repository: `D:\Drive\Source\SquadDash-public`.

Phase 1 (grip strip + hamburger removal):
- Read the spec first
- Remove the hamburger (≡) button from all 5 dockable panel headers (Tasks, Approvals, Notes, Inbox, Maintenance) — search MainWindow.xaml.cs for WirePanelDockingCtrlClick and the hamburger button wiring
- Add grip strip drawing to each panel's header: full-width horizontal lines at top, height = corner radius, alternating drawn/transparent rows, lines shorten near edges to follow corner arc
- Wire a MouseLeftButtonDown handler on the grip strip area that for now just calls ShowDockContextMenu() (the existing context menu) as a placeholder

Phase 2 (DockingMapWindow skeleton):
- Create SquadDash\PanelDocking\DockingMapWindow.cs — a borderless WPF Window (WindowStyle=None, AllowsTransparency=True, Topmost=True, ShowInTaskbar=False)
- Implement BuildDockingMap(string sourcePanelId, PanelLayout currentLayout) returning a DockingMapViewModel with slot button descriptors
- Implement the 6 button generation rules (A–F) from the spec
- Render slot buttons in the three zones (Left columns | Top row | Right columns)
- Wire button clicks to call PanelDockingService.MovePanel and close the popup
- Close on Deactivated event or Escape key

Build: `dotnet build SquadDash\SquadDash.csproj -c Debug`
Test: `dotnet test SquadDash.Tests\SquadDash.Tests.csproj -c Debug`

Commit each phase separately. Report commit SHAs when done.
